name: Gud Cultures LLC
address: 4951 Upper River Rd, Grants Pass, OR 97526

alpha.desc: he alpha view shows the property from the south|Two structures are visible near the roadway at the front of the property|Several large containers are positioned behind the eastern structure|The front portion of the site contains the two visible vehicle entrances and the developed area surrounding the front structures|A line of trees and vegetation separates this area from a second developed section containing three elongated buildings arranged in a row|A paved area is visible near the western entrance and surrounding the three buildings|Trees line portions of the western entrance, and the paved surface transitions into an unpaved roadway extending farther into the property.

bravo.desc: The bravo view shows the property from the west|A combination of paved and unpaved roadways extends from the roadway at the front of the property toward the northern boundary|These circulation routes provide access throughout the length of the site and connect the developed areas with the open field sections|The overall layout of the property and the relationship between the roadways, structures, and field areas are clearly visible from this perspective.

charlie.desc: The charlie view shows the property from the north|The rear-facing sides of the three elongated structures are visible within the central developed area|Trees occupy the northeastern corner of the property, while additional vegetation borders much of the northern property boundary|The open field areas are divided into multiple sections with visible rows present throughout|The rows within the western and eastern field sections generally run north to south, while the rows within the central field section run east to west.

delta.desc: The delta view shows the property from the east|This perspective provides an additional view of the property's overall layout, including the relationship between the structures, circulation routes, and open field area

nadir.desc: The nadir view shows a long, narrow property extending north from the roadway along the southern boundary|Two vehicle access points are visible from the roadway, one near the center of the property between two front structures and a second near the western side of the site|The two structures at the front of the property are separated by a paved area, with an additional paved parking area located behind the western structure|A roadway extends north through the property and connects multiple developed areas|Three elongated structures are arranged in a row within a paved section of the property, with a smaller structure located adjacent to the easternmost building|The remainder of the site consists primarily of open field areas divided into several large sections|Five elongated structures are visible near the center of the property, and multiple additional structures are located along the western side of the site|Circulation routes extend throughout much of the property and connect the developed areas with the field sections.
